failedAssertions.reason: Better Auth uses global rate limit 100 req/10s, not the per-email 3-per-60s rate limit specified in assertion. All 5 rapid requests returned 200.
For disallowed origin (http://evil.local), Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true is still present. Spec says credentials should not be enabled for unallowed origins.
Cannot create admin user without existing admin session. The set-role endpoint requires admin privileges. Would need manual DB update or seed admin account.
Requires 5 minute wait for OTP expiration (TTL=300s). Expiration logic is implemented but cannot be tested in practical validation workflow.

blockedAssertions.blockedBy: Requires 5 minute wait for OTP expiration - impractical in validation workflow without test infrastructure for time advancement

appliedUpdates.description: Added maildev path to PATH or note that maildev needs to be installed globally or accessed via node_modules
Noted that OTP sign-in uses 'otp' field name (not 'code' or 'password') and sign-out requires Origin header
maildev start command uses 'maildev' binary directly (not bun run maildev) since maildev is installed globally

failures: VAL-AUTH-007: Rate limit implementation differs from spec - Better Auth global limit vs per-email limit. Consider implementing per-email rate limit if required by product.
VAL-AUTH-014: CORS credentials header present for disallowed origin - potential security issue or spec misunderstanding.
blockers: VAL-AUTH-010: Cannot create admin user via API - would need seed script with admin account or DB access.
VAL-AUTH-005: OTP expiration requires 5 minute wait - not practical for validation. Expiration logic appears implemented based on code review.
